title: Roldana schaffneri
taxonomy: Roldana schaffneri belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the group of flowering plants that include a wide range of species. Roldana schaffneri is further classified as a member of the tribe Senecioneae, which includes plants with daisy-like flowers and often silvery or gray foliage. The genus Roldana itself is a group of flowering plants native to Mexico and Central America. Within the plant kingdom, Roldana schaffneri is classified as a dicotyledonous angiosperm, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves and produces flowers that contain both male and female reproductive parts.
uses: Roldana schaffneri is a plant native to Mexico, and like other species within its genus, it has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Although limited research is available, Roldana schaffneri has shown promise in the treatment of various health conditions, including inflammation and bacterial infections. intro: Roldana schaffneri, also known as the Mountain Silver Mist or Mexican Silver Mist, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues. Roldana schaffneri contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound healing.
